name: Mister Sunday: Justin Carter All Day Long
description: Justin Carter steps into the booth from open to close. /// Welcome to Mister Sunday. Whether you've been at it with us since day one or you're new to the party, we look forward to seeing you here. All we ask is that you put away your phone and dance while you're on the dance floor. There's plenty of space in the sun and the shade otherwise for a chat and a hang, and we'll have excellent Mexican food for you throughout the party, a wide variety of non-alcoholic and alcoholic drinks at the bar and water throughout the space, always free and easy to access. Tickets are here on RA, and you can RSVP for reduced admission at the link on this page. If joining us is ever a financial hardship for you, drop a line at ask@mistersaturdaynight.com by the Friday before the party, letting us know your situation and what, if anything you can contribute, and we'll do our best to make sure you have a spot. We value your presence more than your money. We're looking forward to dancing with you. /// RSVPers receive $10 off the door charge before 4pm and $5 off before 5pm. An RSVP is good for one person. We stop taking RSVPs an hour before the party starts. In order to receive the RSVP discount, you must be at the ticket desk by the cutoff time and not just in line. It's wise to arrive early to make sure you're not standing in line when the time comes. An RSVP does not guarantee entry. Sometimes parties do sell out, so if you want to be sure you'll get in, it's best to grab a ticket in advance. /// Violence, non-consensual touching, racist, homophobic, transphobic, sexist, ageist or other discriminatory language, and leering are not allowed within our walls or in our back yard. If someone says or does something to make you feel uncomfortable while you’re here, let us know. /// We close the doors of the party at 8:30pm.

name: GUDFELLA with Très Mortimer, Kino Todo & Guests
description: Gray Area returns to Superior Ingredients Roof on Sunday, July 26 for a full day rooftop session in Brooklyn led by GUDFELLA, alongside Trés Mortimer, Kino Todo, and Pardon My French. Born in the Philippines and now based in Los Angeles, GUDFELLA has spent the last few years building momentum through both his productions and his presence behind the decks. With more than 115 million streams and appearances at stages and venues including Coachella, Lollapalooza, Club Space, and Brooklyn Mirage, he has become one of the most talked about names in house music right now. Much of that attention has centered around “Morning Coffee,” a breakout record that found support from artists including Michael Bibi, BLOND:ISH, SG Lewis, Adam Ten, and Carlita. His productions balance warm melodies, rolling percussion, and club focused energy, translating naturally into long form sets that work just as well in open air environments as they do in dark club rooms. Joining the lineup is Chicago native Trés Mortimer, whose sound has earned him releases on labels including Mad Decent, Dim Mak, IN/Rotation, and Ministry of Sound. Alongside launching his own imprint, Optics Records, Mortimer has become a familiar face across both club and festival circuits, with recent appearances at Taste of Randolph, SXSW London, ARC At Sea during Miami Music Week, Mood Ring in New York City, and The Annex in London. His connection to Chicago’s house roots remains central to his sets, balancing deep grooves with melodic moments built for extended dance floors. Also joining the bill is Kino Todo, the project of Tel Aviv artist Yair Raviv. Known for pulling inspiration from different cultures, languages, and textures, Kino Todo first gained international attention through his breakout single “Gidafi Na,” which has surpassed 10 million streams. Since then, he has released music through labels including Disco Halal, Life and Death, Multi Culti, and City Slang, while appearing at events and venues such as DGTL, Circoloco, ADE, Tel Aviv Pride, and The Block. His sets move fluidly between bass heavy rhythms, melodic passages, and globally inspired sounds. Completing the lineup are New York based selectors Pardon My French and Rana Iravani, bringing local support to a day designed to move from daytime grooves into a locked in sunset atmosphere. Running from 2PM through 10PM, Superior Ingredients Roof once again provides the backdrop for another Gray Area rooftop gathering overlooking the Manhattan skyline.

description: Known for his melodic and emotionally driven tracks, Patrice Bäumel’s technical yet warm and atmospheric take on minimal techno is deep, driving, and has carved a unique path through the European and global techno world. He plays all day long in The Nursery. - Public Records aims to provide a safer space. name: Ge-ology, Ayanna Heaven, Cosmo
description: Ge-ology remains one of dance music’s true selectors — a deeply respected DJ, producer, and archivist whose knowledge stretches across house, disco, jazz, soul, hip hop, afrobeat, and experimental electronics. Whether digging through rare groove obscurities or locking into extended dancefloor hypnosis, his sets carry a sense of history, spontaneity, and unmistakable personality that few can match. Ayanna Heaven brings warmth, rhythm, and deep-rooted musicality through sets shaped by house, soul, broken beat, and diasporic groove, while Cosmo rounds out the night with fluid selections that drift between psychedelic house, downtempo textures, and late-night club momentum. RSVP discount ticket is paid at the door. RSVPs are one per person, so anyone coming with you will need to submit their own. name: Refuge Sunday: TEED DJ-Kicks Tour, Hot Honey Sundays, Manguito, Ksenyeah
description: The UK producer, vocalist and songwriter has built a world entirely his own through emotionally charged electronic music moving between house and indie dance. He continues to blur the line between live songwriting and dancefloor energy. Refuge Hi-Def Dance Club is a 500-cap underground nightclub in East Williamsburg built around sound, energy, and atmosphere. At its core is REX, a custom 4-point system designed by co-founder Craig “Shorty” Bernabeu, with four 10-foot stacks surrounding the dance floor for a powerful, immersive experience. Each night is curated to push that system to its full potential — a true Musical Arts Performance in every sense. 21+ only. All sales are final.

name: Playa Peace Project Burning Man Camp Fundraiser: Ali Farahani B2B Shan Nash
description: Playa Peace Project returns to the Playa for their third Burn at Black Rock City. To celebrate the journey and support this year’s vision, join us on Sunday, July 26th at Gospel in NYC for a special fundraiser evening featuring ALI FARAHANI going B2B with SHAN NASH. An intimate night of music, connection, and community in support of bringing Playa Peace Project back home to the dust. PLAYA PEACE PROJECT PPP is a global movement of artists, dreamers, and doers — united by music, generosity, and the spirit of adventure. PPP is a theme camp and international sound collective that spreads peace, unity and positivity through music and the arts at Burning Man and beyond ALI FARAHANI Born in Milan Italy with Iranian heritage, Ali started music when he was 14 years old playing afro cuban percussions. Growing up in Italy, electronic music is ingrained into the culture and has had great influence on the evolution of his productions. Around the age of 18, Ali began to create music. His very first release was titled "Persian Dream" featuring Marco Bonuso released on Sound Division (ITA). Persian Dream was one of the first fusion tracks that truly combined electronic music with Middle Eastern vibes. Persian dream was one of the most successful tracks played in 2007 by some of the largest artists in the world. The next release was a collaboration with DJ Aron of LE Follies De Pigalle called “Don’t feel no shame”. It was featured as one of the top Beatport tracks and number 6 on the overall chart for 2 months in 2008. Shortly after, Ali started to play at many clubs and events across Italy. In 2011, Ali moved to LA and shifted away from European-tech to more sophisticated sounds with experimental grooves and slower beats inspired by my origins. After moving to the US and being away from music for few years, Ali started producing and djing again and most recently opened the Santa Monica based label Pipe & Pochet with Patrik Khach. Ali's goal is to inspire other artists and fans through music and have now began to showcase my productions in the US and beyond. SHAN NASH Shayan Aka Shan Nash Born in Balochestan. Shan Nash’s collective music, part self-produced, captivating other-worldly sounds, part extended live performances that transport audiences to the most majestic and dreamy lands across the globe is the raconteur of his life’s journey. A producer and DJ, Shan embodies the intrinsic characteristics of a true artist: passion, love, empathy and the desire to connect every soul through his calling. A native, a place that still holds his heart yet sought to persecute him for his very deep devotion to music and artistry, Shan’s drawn to the sounds of the East, a signature mark embedded in his art. Since leaving Tehran, a city that embraced his sounds in its deep underground, Shan has found himself in Tulum via L.A. and Istanbul, where he further developed his musical skills. Aside his monthly residency, Shan continues to cast his musical spells across the world through his long-format sets, while continuing to add to his collection of remixes and productions.

name: LightBeam presents AMIL FOREVER: B Hills, Simon Says, sorry i'm late, Sweet Treat
description: This LightBeam gathering is dedicated to the memory of our dear friend Amil Patel. Amil was known and loved by many as an eclectic music lover, musician, a unique + tasteful DJ, and an amazing human being. Jupiter Disco holds special significance for us, as it was the last place we shared an official lineup together - so it’s only right that we run this one back for him properly. Throughout the night, we’ll be celebrating his life and legacy through the music he loved, featuring records from his personal collection and sounds that inspired him. Join us as we honor his spirit, his passion for music, and the everlasting memories he left with all of us. AMIL FOREVER Free all night __________ Jupiter Disco is a safe space for all. Our most important goal is to ensure everyone who steps into our space feels welcome and safe. In order to achieve this goal, it's mission critical that everyone approaches each other with respect. That means everyone. We are passionate about bringing people together and building connections between participants in and adjacent to our community. Go ahead and say hi to someone new while you're here with us! If you see or experience anything that makes you feel uncomfortable, please alert one of our team members and we will coordinate a resolution immediately. We encourage everyone to be present and interface with the physical elements around you while you're with us, so we ask that you please limit your phone use throughout the space. Phone screen and camera lights can also be distracting, so please be mindful of the brightness and the length of time you use those lights, if at all. If you intend to smoke (anything), please take a break outside and take a few steps down the block. Don't worry, we always have re-entry.

With a sound that moves effortlessly between soulful house, organic percussion, and timeless dance music, Pablo crafts sets that are as immersive as they are energizing. Built for close listening and shared moments on the dancefloor, this is a journey where every transition tells a story. Settle into the HiFi and let the music do the talking.
